top_stories.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
top_stories.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign operated without deploying malware, instead silently siphoning authentication tokens to gain unauthorized access to targeted systems.

top_stories.title: The death of standing privilege in the age of AI agents
top_stories.summary: The article addresses the security challenges posed by AI agents functioning as highly privileged identities within enterprise environments. It argues that traditional Privileged Access Management (PAM) controls are insufficient to manage AI agent access, advocating for the elimination of standing privilege in favor of just-in-time access models.

top_stories.title: UNC6783 Hackers Use Fake Okta Pages in Corporate Breach Campaign
top_stories.summary: A threat actor tracked as UNC6783 is conducting a corporate breach campaign by impersonating support staff and using fake Okta login pages to steal credentials and access corporate systems. The campaign combines social engineering with phishing infrastructure mimicking Okta to exfiltrate sensitive data from targeted organizations.

top_stories.title: Privilege Elevation Dominates Massive Microsoft Patch Update
top_stories.summary: Microsoft released a massive patch update addressing 165 vulnerabilities, with privilege elevation bugs accounting for more than half of the total. Two zero-day vulnerabilities were included among the elevation-of-privilege flaws patched in the update.

top_stories.title: Your Next Breach Will Look Like Business as Usual
top_stories.summary: The article addresses the growing threat of credential-based attacks and the detection model shifts cybersecurity teams need to adopt in response. It focuses on how attackers use legitimate credentials to blend into normal activity, making this directly relevant to identity and authentication defense strategies.

news.title: Russia's 'Fancy Bear' APT Continues Its Global Onslaught
news.summary: Russia's Fancy Bear APT group continues conducting global cyber espionage campaigns targeting organizations across various sectors. Security experts cite patching and zero trust architecture implementation as non-negotiable defensive measures against the threat group.

news.title: Anthropic tests user trust with ID and selfie checks for Claude
news.summary: Anthropic has introduced identity verification for its Claude AI chatbot, requiring users to submit government-issued ID and selfie checks, making it the first major AI chatbot platform to implement such a requirement. The verification process is notable in the IAM context as it applies identity proofing techniques to consumer AI access control, while competitors OpenAI and Google have not adopted similar measures.

news.title: FBI and Indonesian Police Dismantle W3LL Phishing Network Behind $20M Fraud Attempts
news.summary: The FBI and Indonesian National Police dismantled the W3LL phishing network, arresting its alleged developer and disrupting infrastructure used to steal account credentials in fraud attempts exceeding $20 million. The W3LL toolkit was an off-the-shelf phishing kit specifically designed to bypass MFA and compromise Microsoft 365 business email accounts at scale.

news.title: Google Rolls Out DBSC in Chrome 146 to Block Session Theft on Windows
news.summary: Google has made Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) generally available to Windows users in Chrome 146, a security feature designed to bind browser sessions to specific devices to prevent session theft. DBSC addresses cookie hijacking attacks by cryptographically tying session tokens to hardware, with macOS support planned for a future Chrome release.

news.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
news.summary: A new phishing-as-a-service platform called "Starkiller" proxies real login pages in real time, relaying victims' credentials and MFA codes to legitimate sites rather than using static page copies. The service effectively bypasses MFA protections by acting as a man-in-the-middle relay between the victim and the authentic website, rendering traditional MFA methods vulnerable.

news.title: TechJutsu launches browser extension to bring MFA to the web
news.summary: TechJutsu has launched a browser extension designed to bring multi-factor authentication (MFA) capabilities to web-based applications. No additional details about the product's specific features, target market, or technical implementation are available from the provided description.

news.title: 5 Ways Zero Trust Maximizes Identity Security
news.summary: Specops outlines five ways Zero Trust architecture strengthens identity security, focusing on limiting access and enforcing device trust. The article addresses stolen credentials as a leading breach vector and covers privilege escalation prevention and lateral movement blocking within an identity-first Zero Trust framework.

news.title: Cisco just made moves to own the AI infrastructure stack
news.summary: Cisco has made moves to strengthen its AI infrastructure stack, including an identity-focused acquisition of Astrix Security. Astrix specializes in non-human identity and API security, addressing machine-to-machine authentication and authorization in agentic AI environments.

news.title: Show HN: Prmana – OIDC SSH Login for Linux with DPoP (Rust, Apache 2.0)
news.summary: A new open-source tool called Prmana has been released that replaces static SSH keys with short-lived OIDC tokens validated through PAM on Linux hosts. The tool differentiates itself by implementing DPoP (RFC 9449), which binds tokens cryptographically to the key holder, built in Rust under Apache 2.0 license.

news.title: Bringing governance and visibility to machine and AI identities
news.summary: AppViewX CEO Archit Lohokare discusses the convergence of machine and AI agent identity management as a unified governance challenge in an interview with Help Net Security. The executive draws on experience from IBM and CyberArk to describe approaches to bringing visibility and governance to non-human identities.

news.title: Identity Protection in the AI Era
news.summary: The article presents a framework for identity-first security that integrates visibility, threat detection, zero trust enforcement, AI protection, and threat intelligence. It addresses how identity protection strategies must evolve in response to AI-era threats.

news.title: Google Chrome Update Disrupts Infostealer Cookie Theft
news.summary: Google has added Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) to Chrome 146, using hardware-bound cryptographic keys to prevent stolen session cookies from being used by infostealers on Windows. DBSC ties authentication sessions to specific devices, meaning stolen cookies cannot be replayed on other machines.

news.title: To counter cookie theft, Chrome ships device-bound session credentials
news.summary: Google is shipping device-bound session credentials in Chrome to counter cookie theft attacks where stolen authentication cookies are used to hijack sessions. The feature binds session credentials cryptographically to a specific device, preventing exfiltrated cookies from being used on attacker-controlled systems.
